description: The deposit and pay-into-firm command paths tell the player "insufficient personal funds" when the real cause is that the firm has no usable Treasury account. FirmTransactionServiceImpl.deposit()/payIn() call resolveAccountId() FIRST (before any funds check), which throws NoFirmAccountException for a firm whose default_account_id is unset or points at an archived/removed account. NoFirmAccountException extends IllegalStateException, so it is swallowed by the generic `catch (IllegalStateException)` and mapped to business.finance.insufficient-personal. The withdraw path (same file, lines 142-145) correctly catches NoFirmAccountException first and shows business.finance.no-account; deposit and payInto do not, so a proprietor with a broken/stale default account is told they are broke instead of that their firm has no account. This is exactly the class of 'error message that misstates the cause' the charter flags.
recommendation: In MiscCommands.doDeposit and MiscCommands.payInto, add a `catch (NoFirmAccountException e) { message.send(sender, "business.finance.no-account"); }` clause BEFORE the `catch (IllegalStateException)` clause, mirroring the withdraw handler at lines 142-145.
verification_note: Verified against all three cited files. NoFirmAccountException.java:13 confirms it extends IllegalStateException. FirmTransactionServiceImpl.deposit() (line 78) and payIntoFirm() (line 402) call resolveAccountId() before any funds check; resolveAccountId (line 283) throws NoFirmAccountException when the firm has no surviving account. In MiscCommands, doDeposit (lines 108-114) and payInto (lines 173-177) catch IllegalStateException -> business.finance.insufficient-personal with NO prior NoFirmAccountException catch, so the no-account condition is swallowed and misreported as 'broke'. The withdraw path (lines 142-145) and doSend (lines 333-334) DO catch NoFirmAccountException first -> business.finance.no-account, proving the inconsistency. Claimed snippet matches verbatim. Minor severity is fair: misleading error message on an edge case (unhealed default/stale/archived account with no survivor), no money-movement or crash impact.

description: disbandFirm/adminDisbandFirm run @Async and are not serialized against each other. Both read the firm, check `archived`, then call disbandInternal, which snapshots the account list and calls firms.archiveFirm(). archiveFirm's UPDATE (FirmMapper.archiveFirm) sets is_archived=1 with no `WHERE is_archived = 0` idempotency guard, so two concurrent disbands (e.g. a proprietor's `/firm disband confirm` racing an operator's `/firm admin disband`, or the public BusinessApi.disbandFirm racing a command) both pass the stale archived check and both snapshot the same positive per-account balance, then each issues a full-balance transfer to the proprietor. Currency is NOT duplicated because the ledger is authoritative: the second treasury.transfer draws from an already-drained account and Treasury's per-account overdraft floor (business accounts default allow_overdraft=false → floor 0) rejects it, and the exception is caught/logged per-account. So conservation holds, but safety here rests entirely on Treasury's floor rather than any guard in Business, and the losing thread logs a spurious drain failure.
recommendation: Guard the disband transition in Business: make FirmMapper.archiveFirm's UPDATE conditional (`WHERE firm_id = #{firmId} AND is_archived = 0`) and have disbandInternal short-circuit (return without draining) when archiveFirm reports 0 rows affected, so only the winning thread performs the drain. This removes reliance on Treasury's floor for conservation of the disband path.
